content:
FINAL EXECUTION TASK — FIX THE ORCHESTRATOR SO COMPLETED TASKS ACTUALLY REACH PRODUCTION

This is an execution task. Do not return narrative, assumptions, placeholders, or simulated deployment reports. Every item below must be completed and verified.

1. Audit every task currently marked COMPLETED.

2. For each completed task return:
   - Task ID
   - Feature
   - Files modified
   - GitHub commit SHA
   - Branch
   - Push status
   - Deployment status
   - Production verification

3. If a completed task never reached production, automatically change its status to NOT DEPLOYED.

4. Audit every BLOCKED task.

5. For every blocked task provide:
   - Task ID
   - Exact blocker
   - Source file
   - Error
   - Whether the block is legitimate
   - Whether the task can continue safely

6. Find the exact reason the orchestrator marks tasks COMPLETE without deploying.

7. Audit the entire deployment chain:
   - Task execution
   - File generation
   - Git commit
   - Git push
   - Deployment trigger
   - Render deployment
   - Health verification
   - Production verification

8. Remove every hardcoded deployment success template.

9. Remove every mock deployment response.

10. Remove every placeholder deployment response.

11. Remove every fallback deployment response.

12. Remove every simulated deployment response.

13. A task may never report COMPLETED until:
   ✓ Git commit exists
   ✓ Push completed
   ✓ Deployment started
   ✓ Deployment completed
   ✓ Health endpoint returns HTTP 200
   ✓ Production is running the latest commit

14. If any requirement fails:
   STATUS = NOT DEPLOYED

15. Repair automatic deployment so every completed task performs:
   Code Change
   → Git Commit
   → Git Push
   → Deployment Trigger
   → Deployment Complete
   → Health Check
   → Production Verification

16. Verify GitHub integration and return:
   - Repository
   - Branch
   - Latest commit
   - Push permission
   - Webhook status
   - Auto-deploy status

17. Verify Render integration and return:
   - Service ID
   - Deployment ID
   - Deployment status
   - Running commit SHA
   - Deployment timestamp
   - Health endpoint
   - HTTP status

18. Verify production is running the newest commit.

19. Compare:
   GitHub HEAD
   vs
   Production running commit

20. If they differ:
   FINAL STATUS = UNVERIFIED

21. Search the entire repository for:
   - Deployment Successful
   - Build Successful
   - Deployment Complete
   - Production Updated
   - Health Check Passed
   - Successfully Deployed
   - Live
   - Verified

22. Replace every fake deployment narrative with real runtime validation.

23. Prevent future fake deployment confirmations by enforcing evidence-first validation.

24. Create automated validation that blocks completion when:
   - No commit exists
   - No push exists
   - No deployment exists
   - No health check exists
   - Production commit differs from GitHub

25. Produce a deployment evidence record for every completed task containing:
   - Task ID
   - Files changed
   - Commit SHA
   - Push timestamp
   - Deployment ID
   - Deployment timestamp
   - Production URL
   - Health URL
   - HTTP status
   - Running commit SHA

26. Execute end-to-end QA after deployment:
   - Landing page
   - Owner login
   - Authentication
   - Chat
   - Reels
   - Properties
   - Media
   - API
   - Database
   - Mobile app

27. Do not report VERIFIED until every QA test passes.

28. Commit all fixes to the production repository.

29. Deploy automatically to production.

30. Return only real production evidence:

- Repository
- Branch
- Commit SHA
- Files changed
- Push status
- Deployment platform
- Deployment ID
- Deployment status
- Deployment timestamp
- Production URL
- Health endpoint
- HTTP status
- Running commit SHA
- QA results

FINAL STATUS MUST BE EXACTLY ONE OF:

VERIFIED

or

UNVERIFIED

Any missing evidence must be reported as UNVERIFIED. Do not replace missing evidence with narrative.
